summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This Java code snippet defines an abstract class `StatusReporter` in the Apache Hadoop MapReduce library. The main purpose of this class is to provide a reporting mechanism for tracking the progress and status of MapReduce jobs.

CORE LOGIC:
The core logic of this class revolves around abstract methods that need to be implemented by concrete subclasses. These methods include:
- `getCounter(Enum<?> name)`: Retrieves a counter by its enum name.
- `getCounter(String group, String name)`: Retrieves a counter by its group and name.
- `progress()`: A method to report progress, which would be implemented to update the status of the job.
- `getProgress()`: Returns a float value between 0.0 and 1.0 indicating the progress of the job.
- `setStatus(String status)`: Sets the status of the job.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
This class does not directly handle inputs or outputs. Instead, it defines methods to report on the status and progress of a MapReduce job, which would be used internally by other components of the Hadoop MapReduce framework. The actual data related to the job's progress or status would be provided or updated by those components when invoking these methods.

DEPENDENCIES:
The key dependencies visible in this chunk are:
- `org.apache.hadoop.classification.InterfaceAudience.Private`: An annotation indicating that this class is part of an internal package and is subject to change without notice.
- `org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.Counter`: An interface representing a named, counted long value, used for tracking job statistics.
- The class itself is part of the `org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce` package, indicating its role within the Hadoop MapReduce framework.

This abstract class serves as a contract for any class that wants to report the status or progress of a MapReduce job, ensuring consistency in the reporting mechanism across different implementations.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The main purpose of this code chunk is to define an abstract class `AbstractCounter` that implements the `Counter` interface, providing common functionality for both `mapred` and `mapreduce` packages.

CORE LOGIC:
The core logic involves the implementation of the `equals` and `hashCode` methods, which are crucial for object comparison and storage in hash-based collections like `HashMap`. The `equals` method checks if two `Counter` objects are equal by comparing their names, display names, and values. The `hashCode` method generates a hash code based on the counter's name, display name, and value, ensuring consistent hashing behavior.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The class does not directly handle any inputs or outputs. Instead, it defines methods and properties (name, display name, and value) that will be set when creating an instance of this abstract class or its subclasses.

DEPENDENCIES:
The code chunk depends on the following classes and libraries:

1. `org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.counters.Counter`: The interface being implemented by `AbstractCounter`.
2. `org.apache.hadoop.thirdparty.com.google.common.base.Objects`: Used for generating consistent and secure hash codes in the `hashCode` method.
3. `org.apache.hadoop.classification.InterfaceAudience.Private`: An annotation indicating that the class is intended for use only within the Hadoop project.

This chunk also imports standard Java classes like `Object` and `String`.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The main purpose of this code chunk is to define the interface `CounterGroupBase`, which serves as a base for counter groups in Apache Hadoop MapReduce. This interface extends `Writable` and `Iterable<T>`, and provides methods to manage counters within a group.

CORE LOGIC:
The core logic revolves around managing counter objects of type `T` (which extends `Counter`) within a group. Key methods include:
- `addCounter(T counter)`: Adds a counter to the group.
- `addCounter(String name, String displayName, long value)`: Adds a counter with a specified name, display name, and initial value.
- `findCounter(String counterName, String displayName)`: Finds a counter by name and display name, creating it if it doesn't exist if the `create` parameter is `true`.
- `findCounter(String counterName)`: Finds a counter by name, creating it if it doesn't exist.
- `incrAllCounters(CounterGroupBase<T> rightGroup)`: Increments all counters in this group by the corresponding counters in the provided group.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The data flowing in involves counter objects of type `T` (which extends `Counter`), their names (`String`), and display names (`String`). The output is the manipulation of these counters within the group, including adding, finding, and incrementing them.

DEPENDENCIES:
The key imported or related classes visible in this chunk are:
- `org.apache.hadoop.classification`: For annotations like `@InterfaceAudience` and `@InterfaceStability`.
- `org.apache.hadoop.io`: For the `Writable` interface.
- `org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce`: For the `Counter` class.
- `org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.counters`: For the `CounterGroupBase` interface itself.
